article_meta.title: Why the US needs China's rare earths16 October 2025ShareSaveAyeshea PereraAsia Digital Editor, Singapore ShareSaveGetty

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several clear instances of author-level bias in the prose. The article has multiple loaded word choices and framing decisions by the author that constitute bias.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model didn't flag anything, so there are no attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so there are no false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Multiple clear author-prose bias instances were missed: (1) 'China has a chokehold on the minerals' - Word Choice/Sensationalism, loaded metaphor by the author; (2) 'tightened its grip' - Word Choice, threatening metaphor; (3) 'dealt a major blow' - Word Choice/Sensationalism; (4) 'precious metal' - rare earths are not metals in the traditional sense, and 'precious' is a subjective qualifying adjective; (5) 'the adversarial tone Trump has struck with them means the US could be left with very few alternative suppliers' - this is Opinion Presented as Fact/Spin, the author presents a causal claim as fact; (6) The overall Slant of the article heavily emphasizes US vulnerability and China's dominance without presenting counterarguments about US diversification efforts already underway; (7) 'Washington has accused China of betrayal' - the word 'betrayal' as characterization of the accusation is loaded framing.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied since nothing was flagged, so this dimension is not applicable. Scoring 1 as there are no incorrect labels.
